ferry.terminal: Multiple CityCat terminals along the river

buses.note: All major bus routes converge in the CBD. The Cultural Centre, King George Square, and Queen Street stations are the main interchanges.

driving.motorway_access: Inner City Bypass, Pacific Motorway, and the Story Bridge converge in the CBD.

primary_state.name: Catchment varies
primary_state.note: Most CBD residential addresses are apartments. Catchments still apply.

secondary_state.name: Brisbane State High catchment for parts

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Queen Street Mall is the central retail spine. Howard Smith Wharves, Eagle Street, and the Riverside precinct anchor the dining experience. The Cultural Precinct sits across the river at South Bank.
lifestyle.river_access: Brisbane River frontage along Eagle Street, City Reach Boardwalk, and Howard Smith Wharves.

pockets.name: Riverside precinct
pockets.character: Apartment stock close to Eagle Street and the river. Premium positioning.

pockets.name: Spring Hill side
pockets.character: Northern CBD streets bordering Spring Hill. Mixed-use.

pockets.name: South Bank-adjacent
pockets.character: Southern CBD streets close to the cultural precinct and the river crossings.

buyer_profile.note: Buyers wanting walk-to-work convenience and inner-CBD lifestyle.
Long-term Brisbane residents moving to apartment living for the convenience and the lock-up-and-leave lifestyle.
Strong rental demand from professionals and students drives the CBD investment market.
